data.title: The whale opened a long position of 1,200 BTC last night, and stopped loss after losing $4.48 million in 17 hours.
data.description:

Data shows that the address opened a long position in BTC at an average price of about $108,700 last night, and then the BTC price continued to fall. In the end, the address chose to stop loss at around $105,000.

data.title: Ethereum's L2 Solutions Address Core Blockchain Challenges
data.description:
According to PANews, with Ethereum's Layer 1 (L1) mainnet gas fees reaching a five-year low, many projects are reconsidering deploying on L1. This raises questions about the core issues that Layer 2 (L2) solutions aim to address.

The blockchain trilemma, as explained by Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin, involves a trade-off between security, decentralization, and scalability. L2 solutions are designed to tackle these challenges by maintaining security through state summaries on L1, striving for decentralization in transaction ordering, and achieving scalability off-chain.

Various L2 solutions, such as Optimistic Rollups and Zero-Knowledge Rollups, are well-known. However, the Based-Rollup approach, initially proposed by Buterin and promoted by projects like Taiko, offers a different perspective. It emphasizes reducing the power of L2 sequencers by allowing Ethereum L1 to handle transaction ordering, thus limiting the influence of L2 sequencers.

In a typical Optimistic Rollup system, sequencers have significant power, deciding transaction order and potentially profiting from Miner Extractable Value (MEV). Different L2 solutions handle MEV differently; for instance, Arbitrum advocates for fair treatment, while Optimism views MEV as a free market activity and taxes it. Despite these differences, L2 sequencers hold a prominent position.

Based-Rollup proposes a three-step process: L2 searchers package transactions and send them to L2 block builders, who then construct blocks. L1 searchers include these L2 blocks in their L1 blocks. This approach allows L1 searchers and L2 builders to be the same entity, utilizing L1 resources to enhance L2 security.

Taiko, which has been developing this approach for a year, is preparing for its token unlock. It has also introduced a new concept called Based Booster Rollup (BBR), which can serve as an L1 mirror. Further analysis of Booster Rollup will be explored in future discussions.

data.title: Whales continue to transfer about $6.47 million of PEPE to Binance, and have realized $5.5 million in profits on PEPE
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Ember's monitoring, a whale transferred 502.5 billion PEPE (about 6.47 million US dollars) to Binance again 20 minutes ago.
The address hoarded 2 trillion PEPE at an average price of 0.00000895 US dollars 22 days ago, costing about 17.9 million US dollars. Since May 9, it has been taking profits one after another. So far, 1.5 trillion PEPE has been transferred to Binance at an average price of 0.0000126 US dollars, with a cumulative value of about 18.95 million US dollars and a profit of about 5.5 million US dollars.
The address currently still holds 497.5 billion PEPE, which is estimated to be worth about 6.41 million US dollars at the current price.

data.title: International Chauffeur Service Webus Plans $300M Raise for XRP Strategic Reserve
data.description:
Webus International, a China-based provider of customizable car and touring services for travelers worldwide, said it plans to raise as much as $300 million through non-equity financing to establish an XRP XRP reserve supporting its global chauffeur payment network.The reserve is part of a broader push to integrate XRP’s cross-border settlement capabilities into Webus’s ecosystem, including on-chain booking records and a Web3-based loyalty program, the company said.The initiative aims to streamline international payments while preserving equity value by relying on loans, credit lines and shareholder guarantees rather than issuing new shares, CEO Nan Zheng said in the statement.The announcement follows Saudi Arabia-based VivoPower International’s plans to build a $121 million XRP treasury, indicative of growing interest from companies in the fourth-largest token by market cap
source: https://www.coindesk.com/business/2025/05/30/international-chauffeur-service-webus-plans-300m-raise-for-xrp-strategic-reserve

data.title: Market Downturn Leads to James Wynn Reducing Long Positions
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, as the market continues its downward trend, prominent investor James Wynn has begun reducing his long positions. Currently, his unrealized losses have increased to $4. 95 million. The details of his positions are as follows: The total value of his positions stands at $167. 12 million, with an opening price of $107,993. 1. The liquidation price is set at $104,530.
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/24934617129409?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Mobility service provider Webus plans to raise $300 million to establish a strategic XRP reserve to support a global driver payment network
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Chinese travel service provider Webus International announced plans to raise up to $300 million through non-equity financing to establish a strategic reserve of XRP and integrate it into the global car-hailing payment network.
Webus will renew its cooperation agreement with Tongcheng Travel Holdings, a large Chinese online travel platform, to expand the "Wetour x Tongcheng" chartered car service route, and plans to use XRP Ledger to settle cross-border itineraries and driver income.
It is reported that Saudi Arabian company VivoPower International also announced plans to establish a $121 million XRP reserve, indicating that the fourth largest crypto asset by market value is gaining more and more attention from companies. Webus's round of financing is still in the intention stage and still requires final agreements and due diligence. (CoinDesk)

data.title: SEC Ends Binance Lawsuit in Major Shift on Crypto Enforcement
data.description:
The U. S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has filed a joint motion with crypto exchange Binance to dismiss their nearly two-year lawsuit, marking the agency’s latest retreat from aggressive crypto enforcement. On May 29, the Commission, Binance, and its co-founder Changpeng Zhao jointly filed a motion in a Washington, DC federal court requesting the dismissal of the SEC’s complaint, which was originally filed in June 2023
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/05/30/sec-ends-binance-lawsuit-in-major-shift-on-crypto-enforcement/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=sec-ends-binance-lawsuit-in-major-shift-on-crypto-enforcement
